purpose: OpenInference-shaped metadata conventions for trace_events.metadata_json in the agent-company control plane.
boundary: This is a convention for local audit metadata, not a full OpenTelemetry exporter.
trace_events.trace_id, lane_id, task_id, agent_id, event_type, event_time, summary, and artifact_path remain the canonical DB columns.
metadata_json carries optional span/runtime/eval/review details until a dedicated observability backend is adopted.

base_fields.span_kind: One of span_kinds. Required for new structured trace events.
base_fields.span_id: Optional local span id. Defaults conceptually to event_id when omitted.
base_fields.parent_span_id: Optional parent event/span id for nested operations.
base_fields.runtime: Local runtime or framework, such as local_static_text_coverage, pydantic_ai_testmodel, openai_agents_sdk, temporal_activity.
base_fields.service_request_id: Required when an event touches gated service-request work.
base_fields.approval_id: Required when an event depends on an approval row.
base_fields.risk_gate: Gate label such as wallet_action, browser_public_action, real_money_trade.
base_fields.source_url: Optional source URL for source refresh or web research events.

llm_fields.provider: Model provider, such as openai, anthropic, local, testmodel.
llm_fields.model: Model id or local test model name.
llm_fields.cost_usd: Observed or estimated model cost. Must be 0 for no-model evals.

tool_fields.tool_args_redacted: Redacted/summary args only. Do not store secrets.
tool_fields.exit_code: Process or tool exit code where applicable.

eval_fields.eval_status: pass, fail, manual_static_pass, needs_model_eval, or blocked.

review_fields.reviewer_agent_id: Reviewer id if local/system review; user id/name only if user explicitly provided it.

rules: Never store secrets, private keys, passwords, cookies, OTPs, or payment details in metadata_json.
Use api_calls=false for dry-run, local static eval, or TestModel events.
Use realized_usd only in outcomes, not trace metadata.
For model events, include prompt_version_id and model/provider before claiming a behavioral eval.
For service actions, include service_request_id, risk_gate, and approval_id when approved.
For public/browser/wallet/legal/security/real-money actions, missing service_request_id means the event is audit evidence of a blocked action, not approval.
